title: 6 x 6 Tales - A Micro Overland Crawl from Jack D
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bT7qGpnOpo4

Daniel (@dungeondive) takes a quick, fond look at 6x6 Tales, a free print-and-play micro overland crawl from Jack D (of Dungeons of D and Adventures of D). It compresses a whole adventure onto two sheets plus a journal page: a simple character, a 6x6 grid you explore by rolling for roads and terrain, dice-and-table combat, quests picked up from your castle and villages, little roll-up dungeon crawls, and a three-week survival clock — all aiming to explore as much of the map as possible before time runs out.

He plays several live turns as "Harlan," delivering scrolls, fighting evolving slimes (week one's pink slimes give way to tougher blue ones, encouraging early levelling), chasing a veteran monster with a regen ability, getting poisoned by bad breakfast, and tallying fame toward a final hero ranking. The game runs entirely on rolling and table lookups, but he loves exactly that low-key, low-stress quality.

It's become his go-to break-time game — a laminated folder kept at work for coffee-break adventures — and he likens it to an even more compact Expedition to Skull Island. Among Jack D's games, this one might be his favourite for sheer ease of setup.

----

Do free, roll-and-lookup micro crawls like this earn a permanent spot in your bag — and what's your favourite "five-minute break" solo game?
